key_findings: persistence_gateway.py events table has no per-sink delivery tracking — sync markers missing; Phase 2 PHASE_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md explicitly requires adding these
Separate sink_outbox table preferred over ALTER TABLE columns on events: avoids lock on 10M+ row table, enables per-sink independent retry, separates ingestion from delivery concerns
event_id (SHA-256 of tool|kind|asset|value|port|source_file|line_no) is the natural idempotency key for all sink writes — already computed by persistence_gateway; reused as FK in outbox
ALREADY_SYNCED is a distinct error class — ClickHouse UNIQUE_VIOLATION and Neo4j ConstraintValidationFailed both indicate the event is already delivered; treat as SYNCED, not FAILED
event_sync_ledger is append-only (immutable receipts) — enables complete delivery audit trail and idempotent replay detection without overwriting state
dead_letter_events preserves raw_json_snapshot at time of final failure — critical for forensic analysis and manual replay; never delete DLQ entries without review
Retry taxonomy covers 11 ClickHouse + 10 Neo4j error patterns with RETRYABLE/NON_RETRYABLE/ALREADY_SYNCED classifications; UNKNOWN errors treated as RETRYABLE with reduced max_retries=2
Backfill queries (commented in migration SQL) populate sink_outbox for pre-existing events at Phase 2 startup without touching the events table

decisions.decision: Separate sink_outbox table, not columns on events
decisions.rationale: Avoids ALTER TABLE on 10M+ row table; per-sink independent retry; clean separation of ingestion vs delivery concerns
ClickHouse and Neo4j fail independently; per-sink rows allow one sink to be SYNCED while the other retries
Immutable audit trail; enables complete delivery history; supports compliance and post-mortem analysis

decisions.decision: ALREADY_SYNCED is a success class, not a failure
decisions.rationale: Duplicate detection at sink (ClickHouse UNIQUE_VIOLATION, Neo4j MERGE find) confirms event exists; marking SYNCED enables correct delivery accounting

decisions.decision: Exponential backoff base=60s, doubles per retry, max 5 retries
decisions.rationale: Base 60s handles transient restarts; 5 attempts = max 960s wait = ~16min total; prevents retry storms on systematic failures

decisions.decision: Dead letter requires human review before replay
decisions.rationale: Automatic replay of malformed payloads would re-fill DLQ; operator investigation ensures root cause is fixed

retry_taxonomy_summary.lookup_method: substring match (case-insensitive) on error message, first match wins

risk_model.risk: Outbox grows unbounded if sinks are permanently down
risk_model.mitigation: Monitor MON-B (oldest pending age); alert if > 2h; auto-escalate to DLQ after max_retries

risk_model.risk: Duplicate writes to ClickHouse on retry
risk_model.mitigation: ALREADY_SYNCED class catches UNIQUE_VIOLATION and marks SYNCED; ClickHouse dedup key = event_id

risk_model.risk: Sync ledger grows to 20M+ rows (10M events × 2 sinks)
risk_model.mitigation: Cold SYNCED rows indexed by synced_at; partition/archive after 30 days; PENDING/FAILED remain hot
Alert on any unreviewed DLQ entry (MON-D); require operator review and replay_requested flag before retry

recommended_actions.action: Run BACKFILL-1 + BACKFILL-2 queries to populate sink_outbox for all existing events
Implement outbox_sweep_worker.py using classify_error() from retry_taxonomy.json
Add MON-F monitoring query to hourly status report
Run idempotency drill: execute sweep twice, verify ClickHouse/Neo4j row counts unchanged
